data.title: Uber is expected to benefit from new investment in Lucid
data.description:
Uber Technologies (UBER) announced an expanded partnership with Lucid Group (LICD) that increases its total investment to $500M and boosts the vehicle purchase commitment to at least 35K Lucid AVs. 

Bank of America thinks Uber's (UBER) additional commitment to Lucid (LICD) signals growing confidence in the company's autonomous vehicle technology, with the companies planning to launch AV services in the Bay Area in the second half of this year. 

"While Waymo city launches and Tesla’s AV progress have made a lot of headlines over the last 6 months, we  are encouraged by Uber’s progress with its US AV initiatives and expect upcoming 2H’26 launches in key AV markets such as Las Vegas (Zoox), LA (Volkswagen), and the Bay Area (Lucid) to serve as potential catalysts for Uber’s stock," noted analyst Justin Post.

Lucid's (LCID) ability to lower the unit cost of AVs to below $50K is considered an important milestone by Post and his team when thinking about competitive positioning against Waymo (WAYMO) and Tesla (TSLA). "We continue to believe increasing visibility on AV production would incentivize other major OEMs to accelerate L4 development, a potential positive for Uber," updated Post.

Bank of America has a Buy rating on Uber (UBER) and a price objective of $103.

data.title: Snapshot of Quant and Wall St. ratings for quantum computing stocks after Nvidia's latest push
data.description:
Quantum computing stocks rallied on Tuesday after Nvidia (NVDA) unveiled a new suite of open-source AI models to tackle key bottlenecks in quantum systems, reigniting investor enthusiasm for the emerging sector.

The NVIDIA Ising is designed to help researchers and enterprises build quantum processors capable of running useful applications. It targets two of quantum computing’s greatest challenges—error correction and system calibration—potentially making quantum machines more practical and scalable.

The rally was broad-based across pure-play names, with IonQ (IONQ) jumping roughly 16%, Rigetti Computing (RGTI) gaining over 10%, and D-Wave Quantum (QBTS) rising about 13%, reflecting heightened optimism that AI could accelerate the commercialization timeline for quantum technologies.

Taking a snapshot of Quant ratings and Wall Street ratings on pure-play quantum computing stocks and also companies that are not pure-play but heavily invested in quantum. 

Pure-play quantum computing stocks:

  • IonQ (IONQ) - Quant rating - 2.62; Wall Street rating - 4.46
  • Quantum Computing (QUBT) - Quant rating - 1.44; Wall Street rating - 4.20
  • Rigetti Computing (RGTI) - Quant rating - 1.44; Wall Street rating - 4.25
  • D-Wave Quantum (QBTS) - Quant rating - 1.28; Wall Street rating - 4.78
  • SEALSQ Corp (LAES) - Quant rating - 1.13; Wall Street rating - 4.50
  • Arqit Quantum (ARQQ) - Quant rating - 1.05; Wall Street rating - 5.00
  • Infleqtion (INFQ) - Quant rating - NA; Wall Street rating - 5.00

Big tech with quantum exposure:

  • Nvidia (NVDA) - Quant rating - 3.48; Wall Street rating - 4.71
  • Microsoft (MSFT) - Quant rating - 3.48; Wall Street rating - 4.71
  • Alphabet (GOOG) - Quant rating - 3.48; Wall Street rating - 4.61
  • Intel (INTC) - Quant rating - 3.46; Wall Street rating - 3.16
  • International Business Machines (IBM) - Quant rating - 3.10; Wall Street rating - 3.86
  • Honeywell International (HON) - Quant rating - 3.09; Wall Street rating - 3.96

data.title: Banks' trading revenues soar amid war-driven market volatility
data.description:
Wall Street's top banks notched new records with their first-quarter results, capitalizing on market volatility fueled by the Iran war.

JPMorgan Chase (JPM) posted its highest-ever trading revenue for the quarter, while Citigroup (C) reported its highest quarterly markets revenue in a decade.

JPMorgan (JPM), Citi (C) and Wells Fargo (WFC) together reported over $25B of profits for the first quarter, as their traders benefited from sharp market moves driven by geopolitical shocks in Venezuela and Iran.

Market volatility is good for investment banks as they make money from financing and facilitating client trades.

Meanwhile, Wells Fargo (WFC) – which is more reliant on retail and commercial banking for its earnings – saw its loan book surpass $1T in the first quarter.

Its CEO Charles Scharf noted that while consumers are spending more than a year ago, including on gas, they haven't slowed spending on everything else yet.

"We have seen historically that it often takes consumers several months to reduce their spend levels on other categories to adjust for higher oil prices," he added. "And while we don't know the exact timing, we would expect to see the same in the second half of the year."

JPMorgan (JPM) CFO Jeremy Barnum also pointed to a "resilient consumer that's doing fine" despite higher gas prices. "We've looked to see if there's evidence of people trading, decreasing other discretionary spending to adjust for higher gas prices, but it's just kind of not enough yet to be visible."

data.title: Bank of America GAAP EPS of $1.11 beats by $0.09, revenue of $30.3B beats by $350M
data.description:
• Bank of America press release (BAC): Q1 GAAP EPS of $1.11 beats by $0.09.
  • Revenue of $30.3B (+10.7% Y/Y) beats by $350M.
  • Revenue, net of interest expense, of $30.3 billion ($30.4 billionFTE),(A) up 7%, reflected higher net interest income (NII), sales andtrading revenue, asset management fees and investment banking fees
  • NII of $15.7 billion ($15.9 billion FTE),(A) up 9%, driven by higher NIIrelated to Global Markets activity, higher deposit and loan balances,and fixed-rate asset repricing, partially offset by the impact of lowerinterest rates
  • Provision for credit losses of $1.3 billion decreased from $1.5 billionin 1Q25 and was relatively flat to 4Q25

data.title: M&T Bank Q1 earnings, revenue beat; issues solid 2026 fee income guidance
data.description:
M&T Bank (MTB) turned in earnings that exceeded the average analyst estimate on Wednesday as it continued to invest in its businesses. Its outlook for 2026 net interest income is roughly in line with consensus, while its guidance for fee income was stronger than the average estimate.

The company expects 2026 net interest income, on a taxable-equivalent basis, to reach $7.2B-$7.35B (vs. Visible Alpha consensus of $7.26B) and fee income of $2.675B-$2.775B (vs. Visible Alpha estimate of $2.66B). GAAP expense is expected to be $5.5B-$5.6B for the year vs. the Visible Alpha consensus of $5.6B.

Q1 operating EPS of $4.18, beating the average analyst estimate of $4.01, fell from $4.72 in Q4 2025 and  rose from $3.38 in Q1 2025.

Q1 revenue of $2.44B, topping the $2.43 consensus, declined from $2.48B in the previous quarter and increased from $2.31B a year ago.

Taxable-equivalent net interest income was $1.76B, vs. the Visible Alpha consensus of $1.77B, dropping from $1.79B in the prior quarter and climbing from $1.71B in the year-ago period.

Noninterest income of $689M dropped from $696M in Q4 and $611M in last year’s Q1.

Provision for credit losses of $140M compared with $125M in the previous quarter and $130M a year ago.

Average loans grew to $138.4B from $137.6B in Q4. Average deposits fell to $164.3B from $165.1B in the prior quarter.

"M&T continued to produce strong operating results and return capital to its shareholders in the recent quarter while investing in its businesses and expanding its operational capabilities in support of our strategic objectives of operational excellence and teaming for growth to meet the needs of our customers and make a difference in people's lives," said Chief Financial Officer Daryl N. Bible.

Conference call at 8:00 AM ET.

data.title: DHT Holdings estimates Q1 time charter equivalent earnings of $78.8K/day
data.description:
DHT Holdings (DHT) estimates time charter equivalent earnings for its fleet at $78,800 per day for the first quarter of 2026, with higher rates of $91,700/day from very large crude carriers (VLCCs) operating in the spot market and $61,300/day from those on time charter.

These estimates are based on 1,994 revenue days during the quarter, of which 1,152 days were spot days.

The estimated TCE earnings on a discharge-to-discharge basis for the company’s VLCCs operating in the spot market, i.e. adjusted for the impact of IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with Customers, were $106,000 per day.

Thus far in the second quarter of 2026, around 49% of the available spot days have been booked at an average rate of $189,500 per day on a discharge-to-discharge basis. In total, 71% of the available revenue days, including both spot and time-charter days, have been booked at an average rate of $115,400 per day.

DHT shares were up 2.1% premarket to $17.55.

data.title: Walmart announces a redesign of its Great Value brand
data.description:
Walmart (WMT) announced on Wednesday that it is launching a comprehensive redesign of its Great Value private brand. It will be the first full refresh of the brand in more than a decade.

The retail giant's goal with the refresh is to make products easier to shop while keeping the same items at the same Every Day Low Prices. As Walmart's (WMT) largest private brand and the largest food and consumables CPG brand in the U.S., Great Value was noted to currently reach nine out of ten U.S. households and save the average family about 35% per year compared with national brands.

The redesign introduces a more modern visual identity and a packaging system focused on both form and function, with consistent placement of nutrition information and benefit claims, clearer visual cues for faster item recognition, and a cohesive look that improves visibility and encourages product discovery across stores and digital channels. Walmart (WMT) highlighted that while the look is changing to better reflect evolving customer preferences and support easier navigation, the products, and pricing inside the packages will remain unchanged.

"Great Value has earned customers’ trust over decades, and while the brand is getting a fresh, modern look, what’s inside isn’t changing. Customers will continue to find the same trusted products at the same Every Day Low Prices they rely on," stated Walmart (WMT) Senior VP Scott Morris.

data.title: First Horizon Corporation GAAP EPS of $0.53 beats by $0.03, revenue of $862M misses by $7.85M
data.description:
• First Horizon Corporation press release (FHN): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.53 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $862M (+6.2% Y/Y) misses by $7.85M.
  • Return on tangible common equity grew to 15.1% in the quarter.
  • Shares +1.4% PM.

data.title: Prediction markets are driving up the cost of acquiring new sports betting customers
data.description:
Prediction market platforms Kalshi (KALSHI), Polymarket (POLYMARKET), Crypto.com OG, and Robinhood Predictions (HOOD) continue to have a significant impact on the online sports betting sector. 

CBRE Equity Research analyst John DeCree noted that BetMGM (MGM) (GMVHF) echoed its peers’ comments that prediction markets could be having a low-single-digit impact on online sports betting handle. Interestingly, the bigger issue right now appears to be the impact on customer acquisition costs.

BetMGM (MGM) (GMVHF) management noted prediction market platforms are investing heavily into sports media, which has some online sports betting operators also bidding up for customer acquisitions and extending payback periods. Expectations for higher customer acquisition costs could show up in the upcoming guidance updates from DraftKings (DKNG) and FanDuel (FLUT) when they report Q1 earnings.

"We suspect media investments from prediction market players will be targeted around key events, including the World Cup and the NFL season, suggesting the competitive environment could get worse before it gets better," warned DeCree.

Of course, DraftKings (DKNG) and FanDuel (FLUT) have their own prediction market initiatives in some states. DraftKings (DKNG) has a standalone app and web product called DraftKings Predictions in 38 states, which lets eligible users trade on real‑world outcomes in sports, finance, and other categories. The product was launched under CFTC oversight via a DraftKings (DKNG) subsidiary that is a registered introducing broker.

Meanwhile, FanDuel operates FanDuel Predicts, a prediction‑market app available in all 50 states that lets users trade on outcomes across sports, economics, financial markets, and crypto. FanDuel Predicts is offered through FanDuel Prediction Markets LLC, a CFTC‑regulated futures commission merchant, and offers event contracts in partnership with CME Group (CME).

data.title: PNC Financial Non-GAAP EPS of $4.32 beats by $0.16, revenue of $6.16B misses by $50M
data.description:
• PNC Financial press release (PNC): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $4.32 beats by $0.16.
  • Revenue of $6.16B (+13.0% Y/Y) misses by $50M.
  • Net interest income of $4.0 billion increased $230 million, or 6%, reflecting the benefit of FirstBank, lower funding costs and commercial loan growth. 
  • Net interest margin increased 11 basis points to 2.95% reflecting an 18 basis point decline in the rate paid on interest-bearing deposits.
  • Provision for credit losses was $210 million in the first quarter and reflected portfolio activity, including loan growth and the addition of FirstBank, as well as updates to macroeconomic factors.  
  •
